remaining_issue_assessment.title: Final verdict transfer semantics must be decision-enforced and config-driven
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: Stage72 now blocks positive final verdicts when transfer confirmation is required but absent, wires the rule into config, and exposes final_decision_use_allowed.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: The current candidate still fails transfer acceptance; the fix prevents false promotion rather than creating a pass.

remaining_issue_assessment.title: Frozen-scope campaign history semantics were misleading
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: Campaign history now records real frozen-scope state and config hash; fail streaks only count matching frozen-scope runs.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: Historical records created before this repair are less informative because they do not all carry the new semantics.

remaining_issue_assessment.title: Runtime truth and canonical-scope blocking were too weak
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: Replay and walk-forward now block on missing resolved_end_ts under frozen scope, and stage67 surfaces runtime_truth_blocked/runtime_truth_reason/effective canonical controls.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: Default config still runs in exploratory mode with frozen_research_mode=false and require_resolved_end_ts=false.

remaining_issue_assessment.title: Cross-perturbation could still run on invalid runtime truth paths
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: Cross-perturbation now checks runtime truth blocking before executing.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: The default run does not exercise a runtime-truth-blocked cross-perturbation path, so the offline evidence is test-backed rather than from the default integration artifact.

remaining_issue_assessment.title: Diagnostics and UI did not expose runtime-truth and final-decision semantics
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: Full trace and the Streamlit pages now show canonical scope, runtime truth blocking, transfer requirement, and final decision authority fields.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: UI exposure is code-verified and artifact-visible, but there is no browser automation test for the Streamlit views.

remaining_issue_assessment.title: Config wiring for transfer confirmation and runtime truth remained incomplete
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: The transfer-confirmation rule is now explicit in config, Stage67 records resolved_end_ts truth controls, and Stage72 consumes the config directly.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: Other legacy config blocks in older stages remain only partially wired.

remaining_issue_assessment.title: Future runs are still exploratory by default
remaining_issue_assessment.implementation_summary: Exploratory-vs-canonical semantics are now explicit in artifacts instead of implicit.
remaining_issue_assessment.remaining_limitations: Canonical mode is still opt-in, so future runs are more interpretable than before but not fully frozen by default.

new_invariants: Stage72 cannot authorize a positive final verdict when transfer confirmation is required but absent.
Frozen-scope campaign fail streaks only count matching frozen-scope runs under the same config hash.
Replay, walk-forward, and cross-perturbation are blocked when runtime truth is invalid under effective canonical controls.
Diagnostics and UI now expose runtime truth, transfer requirement, and final decision authority explicitly.
